name: IRC_CAND_ACTIONS
description: This table contains information about candidate that requires asynchronous confirmation.

columns.Name: PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign Key association with IRC_CANDIDATES table. Required to retrieve (store/delete) the updated Email address for a particular candidate once the candidate confirms the new Email either via mail or CSS.

columns.Name: ACTION_CODE
columns.Comments:
Action specifies the purpose of the row in the new table.
For e.g. If it stores updated email address, then the value would be UPDATE_EMAIL.

columns.Name: ACTION_PAYLOAD
columns.Comments: This is used to store non-PII information.

columns.Name: ACTION_SECURE_PAYLOAD
columns.Comments: This is used to store PII data. For e.g. When new email is entered, the email is stored in this column.

columns.Name: ACTION_STATUS_CODE
columns.Comments: This field along with 'ACTION_PAYLOAD' gives us the current status of a candidate's action.

columns.Name: ACTION_START_DATE
columns.Comments: Stores the timestamp when the Action was triggered. This is one of two columns to track the progress made on the Action.

columns.Name: ACTION_END_DATE
columns.Comments: Stores the timestamp when the Action was completed/withdrawn. This is one of two columns to track the progress made on the Action.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: REQUEST_COUNT
columns.Comments: Counter to track number of times a candidate tries to perform an action.
